strings.app.title: Twitch Studio Native
strings.discord.manual: Discord RPC does not connect automatically. Click "Connect Discord" when you want to start working with Discord.
strings.discord.overlayUnavailable: Local overlay is not running: port {0} is busy or unavailable. Change the port in settings.
strings.members.title: Members
strings.members.previewHint: The buttons below emulate an animation only in the selected member preview.
strings.preview.title: Preview
strings.log.title: Discord RPC Log
strings.hotkey.label: Hotkey: {0}
strings.voice.title: Voice commands
strings.voice.description: Listens to the microphone and starts the selected animation only for the authorized Discord user.
strings.voice.modelDescription: By default the bundled model is used: {0}. You can specify another extracted Vosk model using an absolute path.
strings.voice.useGrammar: Limit Vosk to command phrases only
strings.voice.phraseDescription: A short phrase, for example "hello everyone".
strings.voice.actionDescription: Custom animation name for this phrase.
strings.voice.returnDescription: How many milliseconds after the command to return to the normal Discord/VAD state.
strings.voice.frameDurationDescription: Playback speed for uploaded frames of the selected command.
strings.voice.status.commandNoUser: Command started, but authorized user is unavailable
strings.voice.form.new: Enter a phrase and action name, then save the command.
strings.voice.form.empty: Select or create a command, then upload frames.
strings.voice.form.noAnimation: Animation for this command is not uploaded.
strings.settings.localMicDescription: Local VAD is used only for the authorized user when their Discord microphone is muted. If local mute is enabled, the status remains "Microphone muted".
strings.settings.localVadEnabled: Use local VAD when the Discord microphone is muted
strings.settings.vadModeDescription: RMS uses simple signal volume. Neural mode uses Silero VAD v5 and separates speech from noise better, but uses more CPU.
strings.settings.vadLegend: gray: silence · yellow: transition · green: speech
strings.settings.vadSpeakingThresholdDescription: Signal volume at which VAD starts treating input as speech. Higher values reduce false positives, but may miss quieter speech.
strings.settings.vadSilenceThresholdDescription: Signal volume below which VAD treats speech as finished. Usually lower than the speech threshold so the status does not flicker.
strings.settings.vadAttackDescription: How many milliseconds the signal must stay above the speech threshold before the status becomes "Speaking".
strings.settings.vadReleaseDescription: How many milliseconds the signal must stay below the silence threshold before the status returns to "Idle".
strings.settings.minimizeToTrayDescription: When minimized, the window is hidden and can be restored through the system tray icon.
strings.settings.debugDescription: Shows the log tab and voice command recognition debug blocks.
strings.settings.discordClientDescription: After changing it, click "Connect Discord" again. Your Discord app must be enabled as a Public Client.
strings.settings.portDescription: Local HTTP server port for the OBS source and preview. If the port is busy, choose another one and save.
strings.settings.portInvalid: Port must be a number from 1 to 65535.
strings.settings.overlayStopped: Overlay server is not running. Check whether port {0} is free.
strings.settings.discordClientSaved: Discord Client ID saved. Click "Connect Discord" to connect with the new application.
strings.settings.discordClientAlreadySaved: Discord Client ID is already saved.
strings.settings.discordClientInvalid: Discord Client ID must be a numeric Discord application ID.
strings.settings.vadWaitingDiscordMute: Waiting for muted Discord microphone on the authorized user
